evidence_scope.causal_boundary: Only exact matched comparisons over structured I1 fields may support the scoped memory-effect claim.
evidence_scope.purpose: Choose whether a bounded retention cohort should remain staged after a public-data return-rate signal.
evidence_scope.source: Public UCI Online Retail II dataset scenario, DOI 10.24432/C5F88Q

limitations: The deterministic matrix tests contract semantics, not general model quality.
The authoritative live route is the existing supported Codex subscription evidence; no new provider work was started.
Beneficial impact, reliability, and longitudinal quality remain gated on L1 outcome evidence.

with_context: fresh cohort evidence contradicts the signal

limitations: A matched deterministic receipt supports only the scoped memory-effect claim.

reconsideration_conditions: fresh cohort evidence contradicts the signal

impact.boundary: Material influence is not beneficial impact; benefit requires later L1 outcome evidence.

unsupported_claims: A reflected identifier or copied wording proves materiality.
Cross-provider, cross-model, or configuration-mismatched differences prove a memory effect.
Decision-material retained intelligence is necessarily beneficial.
I3 establishes the later L1 beneficial-impact claim.
